channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Ubisoft shares plunge 16% after Assassin’s Creed maker warns of annual profit loss
item.description: Ubisoft shares plunged on Thursday after the Assassin's Creed maker warned of further losses this year.

item.title: Nvidia says it has ‘largely conceded’ China’s AI chip market to Huawei
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the company has "largely conceded" China's advanced artificial intelligence chip market to Huawei.

item.title: Iran reviews U.S. peace proposal as Trump says he’s willing to wait 'a few days'
item.description: It comes after President Donald Trump said he was prepared to wait a few more days to "get the right answers" from Tehran.

item.title: Three key takeaways from Putin's Beijing trip — and what they reveal about China-Russia ties
item.description: Putin left Beijing with declarations of enduring friendship with China and a stack of bilateral agreements — but without the energy breakthrough Moscow desperately sought.

item.title: Inflation fears are rippling through industrial metals. Here's where prices could go next
item.description: The moves came amid broader volatile trade in global bond and equity markets, faced with fears of resurgent inflation.

item.title: From SALOs to SMIDs: How investors are trading the UK as political turmoil rattles British markets
item.description: British assets have been volatile in recent weeks, with Prime Minister Keir Starmer's leadership under pressure.

item.title: Treasury yields resume climb as traders monitor inflation risks
item.description: Borrowing costs edged higher Thursday as investors eye ongoing inflationary pressures in the U.S. economy.

item.title: SpaceX's historic IPO plans: Billions in losses and Musk's massive ownership
item.description: SpaceX filed for an IPO and plans to list on Nasdaq under ticker SPCX, according to its SEC prospectus.

item.title: SoftBank Group shares soar 20% as Nvidia earnings signal strong AI momentum
item.description: SoftBank Group is closely tied to the AI boom through its stake in Arm Holdings, whose chip designs are used in AI servers and data centers powered by Nvidia systems.

item.title: Jeff Bezos says there is ‘no truth’ to the ‘buy borrow die’ tax strategy
item.description: The "buy, borrow, die" strategy refers to the practice of wealthy founders or investors borrowing against their assets and using the loan proceeds as income.

item.title: Nvidia earnings takeaways: Data center revenue nearly doubles, report is strong but stock slides
item.description: On the earnings call with analysts, CEO Jensen Huang explains the reason behind the chipmaker's reporting change.

item.title: College career path ‘over’ as skilled trade get 30% pay bump, recruitment giant says
item.description: "I would say you can make a good career and good money in skilled trade. That's definitely a career track," CEO of world's largest recruitment firm told CNBC.

item.title: Jim Cramer says the world of tech investing has changed and it's not going back
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said semiconductor and AI infrastructure stocks have replaced software as the market's technology leaders.

item.title: Bezos brushes off concerns of an AI bubble: 'You shouldn't worry about it'
item.description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos shrugged off concerns of a looming AI bubble, saying the massive investments will ultimately drive the technology forward over time.

item.title: Bezos defends billionaires, hypes AI, talks taxes and praises Trump in CNBC interview
item.description: Jeff Bezos rejected accusations that Amazon's decision to release a documentary on first lady Melania Trump was an effort to curry favor with the president.

item.title: AI frenzy sees these European stocks gain over 100% this year in blockbuster rally
item.description: Europe has long lagged behind the U.S. and China in AI, but these stocks are riding the wave of investor interest.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: India’s investment appeal dims as firms and funds pivot to the U.S.
item.description: AI-driven growth and 'America-first' policies are pulling global capital to the U.S., challenging India's appeal as the fastest-growing economy.

item.title: Japan’s megabanks post record profits, but analysts warn growth may slow as risks mount
item.description: Japan's largest lenders may face some challenges to keep profit levels at record highs, amid higher credit costs and current geopolitical risks

item.title: Europe's biggest SpaceX rival rockets 10% as markets trade lower
item.description: European stocks are expected to open around the flatline on Thursday as investors assess the latest macroeconomic and geopolitical news.

item.title: Japan exports jump 14.8% in April, beating expectations as semiconductor shipments soar
item.description: The figure outperformed the 9.3% estimate from Reuters.

item.title: What Goldman’s lead role in SpaceX’s record-breaking IPO means for investors
item.description: It's a major win for Goldman's dealmaking division.

item.title: Considering the Chase Sapphire Reserve's 150,000-point bonus? Here are 4 easy-to-miss reasons it's a great deal
item.description: The Sapphire Reserve is more than just a big bonus. Carefully considering these factors can help you easily determine if it's a good fit for you.

item.title: Why some investors are turning to high-yield bonds amid the volatility. Where to find opportunity
item.description: Rates recently took a leg higher across the fixed income market, but some investors see opportunity in high-yield bonds.

item.title: Congress is best chance to stop Trump 'lawfare' fund, attorneys say
item.description: The Department of Justice has set up a $1.8 billion fund that will purportedly compensate victims of prosecutorial overreach under the Biden administration.

item.title: Thursday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: The S&P 500 snapped a three-day losing streak on Wednesday as Treasury yields and oil prices retreated.

item.title: Intuit plans to cut workforce by about 17% as tax software maker reckons with slowing growth
item.description: Intuit's stock has been hammered this year as investors worry that generative artificial intelligence models could threaten software companies.

item.title: SpaceX is poised to be the biggest IPO ever. Here are the top U.S. deals to date
item.description: Before SpaceX's upcoming IPO sets an expected record, Alibaba, Facebook and Visa raised historic sums in the U.S.

item.title: E.l.f. Beauty to walk back some tariff price increases amid high gas prices and consumer 'suffering'
item.description: E.l.f. Beauty raised prices by $1 last August to offset the impact of tariffs. It's now planning to walk back some of that amid a consumer pullback.

item.title: Sinkhole shuts runway at LaGuardia Airport, delaying flights, Port Authority warns
item.description: The Port Authority said travelers should expect delays and cancellations, with thunderstorms expected also expected to roll in Wednesday.

item.title: OpenAI takes the lead in AI IPO horse race: 'Getting to public markets first is very important'
item.description: It comes after reports that the AI giant and ChatGPT owner is set to file confidentially for an IPO as soon as this week.
